meta.title: Web Caching – Improve Speed with Browser & CDN Caching (2026)
meta.description: Learn web caching techniques including browser caching, CDN caching, and cache control headers to improve performance.

content.value: Web caching is a technique that stores copies of resources like HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and images so they can be loaded faster on subsequent requests.
Caching reduces server load, speeds up page loading, and improves user experience by serving stored resources instead of fetching them again.

rows: Stores data on server for reuse

content.value: Cache-Control defines how resources are cached and for how long.
These headers help browsers determine if a resource has changed and whether it should be reloaded.
Content Delivery Networks cache content closer to users, reducing latency and improving load times.

value: **Use long cache duration for static assets**

content.value: Web caching is a powerful technique to improve performance. Proper caching strategies reduce load time, save bandwidth, and enhance user experience.

faq.question: What is browser caching?
faq.answer: It stores website resources locally in the user's browser.

faq.question: What is CDN caching?
faq.answer: It caches content on distributed servers closer to users.

faq.question: What is cache-control?
faq.answer: It defines caching rules for resources.
